symptom: Azure Automation scheduled jobs cannot load cmdlets in certain PS modules. Cmdlets show not recognized when scheduled jobs are processed, but triggered jobs work fine.
rootCause: Modules used for scheduled jobs depend on when the schedule was created, not when the scheduled job ran. Newly imported modules are not picked up by existing schedules.
solution: Create a new schedule after new PS modules are uploaded, and link the new schedule to existing runbooks.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======16. Automation======/16.1 Azure Automation scheduled job cannot load ce.md

symptom: Automation runbook script stopped unexpectedly and restarted from the start without any error. Jarvis shows sandbox crashed.
rootCause: Script consumed 400MB+ memory which exceeded the Azure Automation sandbox memory limitation.
solution: Split the script into small runbooks and call them in separated jobs using child runbook pattern (Start-AzureRmAutomationRunbook -wait).
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======16. Automation======/16.2 [Automation] Respect service limitation and m.md

symptom: Parent runbook reports sub runbook not found error after the first few subscriptions when calling child runbooks in a foreach loop.
rootCause: Select-AzureRmSubscription (Set-AzureRmContext) in child runbooks changes session-wide subscription context in the parent runbook, causing it to lose access to the Automation Account subscription.
solution: Add Select-AzureRmSubscription cmdlet at the beginning of each foreach iteration in parent runbook to reset subscription context back to Automation Account subscription.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======16. Automation======/16.2 [Automation] Respect service limitation and m.md

symptom: Automation Runbook gets 403 Forbidden error when accessing Storage Account with Firewall enabled, even after adding Automation service tag IP ranges to Storage firewall rules.
rootCause: Automation service tag IP ranges are for accessing JRDS, Agent service, or Webhook only. Cannot be used to access Storage from runbook jobs.
solution: Use Hybrid Runbook Worker: (1) Install hybrid worker on VM. (2) Enable Storage Service Endpoint on worker VNET or add hybrid worker IP to Storage firewall. (3) Schedule runbook on the hybrid worker group. For Mooncake, modify New-OnPremiseHybridWorker.ps1 for correct environment.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======16. Automation======/16.3  [Automation] Connect an Azure Runbook to Sto.md

symptom: PS modules imported to Automation Account are not recognized inside script blocks when running PowerShell Runbook on Azure sandbox. Start-Job with ScriptBlock fails to find modules.
rootCause: PowerShell Runbook on Azure only supports serial execution. Script blocks run in a separate process where Automation Account modules are not available.
solution: Use Hybrid Runbook Worker for parallel execution with script blocks. On hybrid workers, locally installed PS modules are available inside script blocks. Pass parameters via -ArgumentList.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======16. Automation======/16.4 [Automation] Some key learnings on Automation.md

symptom: Set-AzStorageBlobContent fails with Illegal characters in path error when running runbook on Hybrid Worker, even though the path is correct.
rootCause: Missing Orchestrator.Sandbox.exe.config file on the Hybrid Worker. Known issue: Azure PowerShell GitHub #8531.
solution: Run PowerShell script on Hybrid Worker to create Orchestrator.Sandbox.exe.config with AppContextSwitchOverrides value Switch.System.IO.UseLegacyPathHandling=false. Note: config may be removed on agent auto-update, requiring reapplication.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======16. Automation======/16.4 [Automation] Some key learnings on Automation.md

symptom: Linux VMs not showing in Update Management Solution Dashboard despite being connected to Log Analytics workspace. Windows VMs show up immediately but Linux VMs do not.
rootCause: For Linux, Update Management uses hybrid worker component requiring Automation Account. UpdatesEnabled is only True when VM is added through Automation Account > Update Management. Windows uses MMA agent intelligence packs and does not need this.
solution: Add Linux VMs through Automation Account > Update Management page. Wait for UpdatesEnabled to become True and VM to appear in Solution Dashboard.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======16. Automation======/16.6 Update Management Dashboard显示不全.md

symptom: Automation runbook using Update-AutomationAzureModulesForAccount.ps1 to update Az modules runs timeout after 3 hours with no error in job stream log.
rootCause: Two issues: (1) Script deadlocks when dependent Az modules do not exist (e.g., Az.MySql in old versions). (2) Set-AzAutomationModule cmdlet hits known issue (GitHub #16399) cannot upload PS 7.1 modules.
solution: Use REST API to replicate portal Update Az Modules action: capture F12 browser trace to get exact API calls. Use PATCH method with RuntimeConfiguration payload. Enable MSI on automation account for authentication.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======16. Automation======/16.7 How to use REST API to do azure portal operat.md

symptom: 403 Forbidden when using Billing API (Microsoft.Subscription/aliases) to create Azure subscriptions via Managed Identity of Automation Account. User account with same Contributor permission succeeds.
rootCause: Workload DevTest in request body is not a valid SKU ID for the invoice section. Customer billing account only has skuId 0001 (Microsoft Azure Plan / Production) enabled. The 403 error message about insufficient permissions is misleading.
solution: Change workload from DevTest to Production in API call body. Check enabledAzurePlans via ListInvoiceSectionsWithCreateSubscriptionPermission API to verify available SKU IDs before creating subscriptions.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======24. Billing API (21v Customer Agreement)====/24.1 Received 403 forbidden when using billing API.md

symptom: Azure Resource Inventory (ARI) automation runbook fails in Mooncake. Runtime Environment option required by ARI docs does not exist in Mooncake Automation Account. Adding modules via Modules option instead leads to import command failure.
rootCause: ARI's automation setup requires Runtime Environment feature which is not fully supported in Mooncake. Module import within runbook script fails due to environment limitations.
solution: Use PowerShell locally instead of Automation runbook: Install-Module -Name AzureResourceInventory; Import-Module AzureResourceInventory; Invoke-ARI -TenantID xxx -SubscriptionID xxx -AzureEnvironment AzureChinaCloud. Generates Excel inventory report.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AUTOMATION/## Case Study/Azure Resource Inventory.md

symptom: Automation runbook job restarted unexpectedly and eventually went to Stopped state. EtwJobStatus shows job Running then Stopped with sandbox change.
rootCause: Two factors: (1) Sandbox process crashed due to transient infra issue (DrawbridgeHostV1 shows 'Process exited with non-zero code 0xffffffff'). Automation retry mechanism restarted the job on a new sandbox. (2) Customer manually stopped the restarted job via POST /AUTOMATIONACCOUNTS/JOBS/STOP (confirmed via ARM HttpIncomingRequests with clientApplicationId and principalOid).
solution: (1) Design runbooks to be idempotent and support restarts per fair-share doc. (2) For hybrid workers, develop runbooks that handle unexpected restarts. (3) Use EtwJobStatus + DrawbridgeHostV1 + EtwIncomingWebRequest + ARM HttpIncomingRequests to trace full lifecycle of job state changes.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AUTOMATION/## Case Study/[Runbook] restarted and went to stopped state fina.md

symptom: Linux VM disappears from Update Management. Hybrid worker ping stops in Kusto. UM_Linux_Troubleshooter_Offline.py reports 'Hybrid worker is not running'. nxOMSAutomationWorker module directory missing. Reinstalling omsagent and toggling UM does not help.
rootCause: CROND service was disabled on the Linux VM. PerformRequiredConfigurationChecks.py (which pulls DSC config from Log Analytics and installs/starts nxOMSAutomationWorker) is triggered by cron every 15 minutes. Without cron, the worker manager never starts and modules are never installed. Omsagent reinstall does NOT trigger PerformRequiredConfigurationChecks.
solution: Enable crond: systemctl enable crond; systemctl start crond. Alternatively, manually run: sudo -u omsagent python /opt/microsoft/omsconfig/Scripts/PerformRequiredConfigurationChecks.py. Tips: omsagent heartbeat confirms agent state; hybrid worker heartbeat confirms nxOMSAutomationWorker state; PerformRequiredConfigurationChecks must run as omsagent user.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AUTOMATION/## Case Study/[UM] VM disappear due to cron issue.md

symptom: Linux VM not showing in Update Management schedule update deployment preview. VM exists in workspace but hybrid worker processes (oms.conf/worker.conf) are not running. IMDS endpoint (169.254.169.254) returns no output on the problem VM.
rootCause: Proxy configuration on the Linux VM blocks access to IMDS endpoint 169.254.169.254. IMDS does not support proxy and requires direct access. Without IMDS, the VM cannot properly register with Update Management.
solution: Add no_proxy=169.254.169.254 to the Linux proxy configuration. Verify by running: Invoke-RestMethod -Headers @{'Metadata'='true'} -Method GET -Uri http://169.254.169.254/metadata/instance?api-version=2020-09-01 | ConvertTo-Json. Check hybrid worker processes are running after fix.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AUTOMATION/## Case Study/[UM]VM disappear due to proxy issue 21090300400014.md

symptom: Runbook with cmdlets containing -Debug parameter hangs indefinitely. The runbook never completes because Azure Automation sandbox cannot handle interactive confirmation prompts.
rootCause: Cmdlets with -Debug switch trigger interactive ShouldContinue prompts. Azure Automation sandbox has no interactive console to respond, so the runbook blocks forever.
solution: Set $GLOBAL:DebugPreference="Continue" at the start of the runbook to suppress interactive prompts. Redirect debug stream to output stream using 5>&1 (e.g., Get-AzSqlElasticPool ... 5>&1) so debug messages appear in job output. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/general/capture-debug-stream
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AUTOMATION/## Common Code Samples/How to handle -debug interaction prompt in runbook.md

symptom: VMs in non-chinaeast2 regions (e.g. chinanorth, chinanorth3) cannot onboard to Update Management (AUMv1) from the Azure portal Add Azure VM or VM Updates page in Mooncake.
rootCause: Update Management in Mooncake is only GA in chinaeast2 region. The portal onboarding UI for other regions is not functional.
solution: Associate non-chinaeast2 VMs with a Log Analytics workspace in chinaeast2 region. Then register VMs via the chinaeast2 Automation Account > Update Management > Manage machines option. VMs can be managed cross-region through the chinaeast2 automation account.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AUTOMATION/## Feature releasement and verification/Update management(AUMv1).md

symptom: Change Tracking and Inventory feature is not visible in Automation Account blade in Mooncake portal. Customer expects to configure Change Tracking from Automation Account.
rootCause: In Mooncake, Change Tracking only works for Windows/Linux files and Windows registry through Security Center File Integrity Monitoring (FIM). The feature is not surfaced in the Automation Account UX.
solution: Use Microsoft Defender for Cloud > File Integrity Monitoring (FIM) to access Change Tracking functionality for Windows/Linux files and Windows registry in Mooncake. Direct Automation Account integration is not available.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AUTOMATION/## Feature releasement and verification/## Feature List and Gap.md

symptom: PowerShell Modules Gallery is not accessible from Mooncake Automation Account. Cannot browse or import modules from gallery. Start/Stop VM solution and Event Grid integration also not visible in Automation Account.
rootCause: Mooncake Automation Account does not include Modules Gallery, Start/Stop VM solution (N/A), and Event Grid integration (GA but not visible in Automation Account blade). These are feature gaps between Public Azure and Mooncake.
solution: For modules: manually download from PSGallery and upload via portal or REST API (see automation-onenote-008 for REST API approach). For Start/Stop VM: implement custom runbook. For Event Grid: configure via Event Grid service directly, not through Automation Account blade.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AUTOMATION/## Feature releasement and verification/## Feature List and Gap.md

symptom: Update Management online troubleshoot script link does not work on Mooncake (learn.microsoft.com/zh-cn/azure/automation/troubleshoot/update-agent-issues-linux#start-the-troubleshooter)
rootCause: The UM troubleshoot link is not available in Mooncake environment
solution: Use Azure Portal: VM > Updates > Troubleshoot button to run online diagnostics. Alternatively, run offline script: https://learn.microsoft.com/zh-cn/azure/automation/troubleshoot/update-agent-issues-linux#troubleshoot-offline
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AUTOMATION/## Troubleshooting/Hybrid worker/Online troubleshoot script for UM.md

symptom: Runbook job failed or suspended unexpectedly. Need to diagnose sandbox-level root cause via Jarvis.
rootCause: Sandbox process terminated due to memory exhaustion (400MB+ limit) or other resource constraints. Identified via Jarvis EtwAll (SandboxHandleJobActionEnter) + DrawbridgeHostV1 queries.
solution: 1) Get Job ID from portal. 2) Query EtwAll with TaskName=SandboxHandleJobActionEnter to get processId. 3) Query DrawbridgeHostV1 with pid to find termination reason. If memory exhaustion, split script into smaller runbooks.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AUTOMATION/## Troubleshooting/Jarvis/How to find sandbox related events for a failed_su.md

symptom: Cannot create Automation Account with RunAs Account — Add blade immediately shows warning 'insufficient privileges' and Create button is blocked
rootCause: User is a Guest (not Member) in the subscription's home Azure Active Directory. Guests have minimal rights and cannot create service principals required for RunAs accounts.
solution: Global Admin converts user from Guest to Member: Connect-MsolService; (Get-MsolUser -SearchString '<user>') | Set-MsolUser -UserType 'Member'. Then retry Automation Account creation.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AUTOMATION/## Troubleshooting/RunAs Account issue/1. Add Automation Account immediately blocks due t.md

symptom: Azure RunAs account (service principal) creation fails during Automation Account deployment with 'Authorization_RequestDenied: Insufficient privileges to complete the operation'
rootCause: App Registration setting in Azure AD User Settings is set to 'No' by Global Admin, preventing non-admin users from registering applications/service principals.
solution: Global Admin re-enables App Registration in Azure AD > User Settings, or assigns the user to an appropriate AD role with application registration permissions.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AUTOMATION/## Troubleshooting/RunAs Account issue/2. Azure Run As account (service principal) creati.md

symptom: Runbook fails with 'Error validating credentials. Password is expired' or '401 Unauthorized' after working fine for months
rootCause: OrgId credentials (Azure AD username/password) expire every 90 days by default. The password stored in Automation credential asset becomes stale.
solution: 1) Reset the user password in Azure portal. 2) Login with the account to complete password update. 3) Update the password in Automation Account > Credentials asset to match new password.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AUTOMATION/## Troubleshooting/Runbook common troubleshooting/Error validating credentials. Password is expired..md

symptom: Runbook job fails with 'The quota for the monthly total job run time has been reached for this subscription'
rootCause: The Automation free tier monthly job run time quota has been exhausted. The Pricing Tier and Usage blade may show minutes below the limit due to display lag.
solution: Upgrade the Automation Account pricing tier temporarily (Pricing Tier and Usage > select higher tier). Can be downgraded back later. Alternatively, wait until next month when quota resets.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AUTOMATION/## Troubleshooting/Runbook common troubleshooting/Job quota is reached.md

symptom: Windows 'updates for other Microsoft products' setting keeps getting disabled after being enabled via Group Policy. AUM resets the setting.
rootCause: Azure Update Manager (AutomaticByPlatform mode) modifies the registry setting for Windows Update, disabling updates for other Microsoft products. This is by-design behavior documented in automatic VM guest patching docs.
solution: Use ServiceManager PowerShell script on the server: $ServiceManager = (New-Object -com 'Microsoft.Update.ServiceManager'); $ServiceManager.AddService2('7971f918-a847-4430-9279-4a52d1efe18d',7,''). For Server 2016+, use Group Policy Administrative templates. Verify with HT wiki: Check what patch source is configured.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Update Manager/## Case Study/Enable updates for other Microsoft products.md

symptom: Customer received notification about AUM billing for Azure Arc-enabled servers in Mooncake, but they are not using Arc-enabled servers
rootCause: PG mistakenly sent the notification to ALL AUM customers in Mooncake on July 25th, not just Arc-enabled server users. The billing only applies to customers using AUM for Arc-enabled servers.
solution: Inform customer this was an incorrect notification from PG. The billing is only for AUM on Arc-enabled servers. Contact AUM PG (Aakash Panchal) if customer has more questions.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Update Manager/## Know issue/AUM Azure Arc-enabled servers mis-leading message.md

symptom: Cannot change VM patch orchestration mode to 'Manual' or 'AutomaticByPlatform' after VM deployment in Mooncake. Portal does not show the option.
rootCause: The patchSettings.patchMode ARM property can only be set at VM deployment time and cannot be changed without redeploying the server. It sets the NoAutoUpdate registry key at deployment.
solution: Redeploy the VM with the correct patchMode in ARM template (osProfile.windowsConfiguration.patchSettings.patchMode=AutomaticByPlatform). Local registry changes will be out of sync with ARM view. For Linux: osProfile.linuxConfiguration.patchSettings.patchMode=AutomaticByPlatform.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Update Manager/## Know issue/Cannot enable orchestration with Manual in Mooncak.md

symptom: Need ICM template link for Azure Update Manager escalation
solution: AUM ICM template: https://portal.microsofticm.com/imp/v3/incidents/create?tmpl=ja3O2a
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Update Manager/## ICM Template.md

symptom: Azure Update Manager does not support Windows Client OS (e.g., Windows 10/11). Customer expects AUM to manage client machines.
rootCause: AUM only supports Windows Server and Linux Server OS versions listed in the support matrix. Windows Client OS is explicitly listed as unsupported workload.
solution: Refer customer to support matrix: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/update-manager/support-matrix?tabs=21via,azurevm-os. For unsupported workloads: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/update-manager/support-matrix?tabs=21via,azurevm-os#unsupported-workloads. Use alternative patching solutions (WSUS, Intune) for client OS.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Update Manager/## Know issue/Windows Client is not supported.md

symptom: Customer's VM image is not supported by Azure Update Manager (AzGPS). Assessment or patching operations fail or show unsupported.
rootCause: The VM image is not in the AUM supported images list. If the OS vendor still supports the OS, PG may add support upon request.
solution: 1) Submit New Image Request form: https://forms.microsoft.com/pages/responsepage.aspx?id=v4j5cvGGr0GRqy180BHbR8SQCvBHdBRGmoav-MUl5-hUNjdTUDNRWEI2TkU0Wk1STUNLVzRKREkwMS4u. 2) Email aumpm@microsoft.com and aumeng@microsoft.com with Product/Offer/Sku + impacted VM count. 3) If no response, raise ICM: https://portal.microsofticm.com/imp/v3/incidents/create?tmpl=X1t346. NOTE: Do NOT request support for vendor-EOL OS (e.g., RHEL 6, CentOS 8).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Update Manager/## TSG/TSG_ Is my machine supported for Azure Update Mana.md
